items.title: Man charged with death threat to woman in Kota Kinabalu court
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, May 16 — A 21-year-old man was charged in the Magistrate’s Court here with threatening to cause death to a woman. Meanwhile, a 41-year-old man was fined RM2,800 or six months’ jail for keeping a stolen wall fan. Maarof Garib pleaded guilty to having the stolen wall fan belonging to Shell Station Wisma Innoprise, Jalan UMS, at the male surau of the petrol station at about 12.30pm on May 9.

items.title: Federal Territories PKR Youth chief quits party
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Federal Territories PKR Youth chief has quit the party, citing the party's drift from the reform principles he had believed in since his days as an activist. He announced his resignation from all party positions effective immediately. Asheeq said that his departure from the party would not affect his commitment to the Batu community.

items.title: Anwar to teachers: Stay positive in face of criticism
items.summary: He said teachers must continue to play a central role in shaping students' character and values, despite being increasingly affected by social issues that spill into schools. "Teachers appear to be burdened. Also present were Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi, Chief Secretary to the Government Tan Sri Shamsul Azri Abu Bakar, Education Minister Fadhlina Sidek, Perak Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Saarani Mohamad, and Datuk Seri Dr Wan Azizah Wan Ismail.

items.title: Subsidy reforms not strictly reliant on income classification, says Economy Minister
items.summary: Economy Minister Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir said the ministry avoids rigid classifications such as "T20", as household income alone does not fully reflect economic reality. Akmal said subsidy rationalisation remains part of the government's wider economic restructuring agenda aimed at ensuring assistance is delivered more effectively to the intended groups. Akmal said discussions on more targeted subsidy distribution are ongoing, particularly to ensure government programmes reach their intended beneficiaries.

items.title: Driver loses control, crashes car into Kuching coffee shop, two sent to hospital
items.summary: KUCHING, May 16 — An elderly man lost control of his sedan and crashed into a coffee shop near Jalan Rock traffic light junction here today. See Hua Daily News reported that the incident occurred at around 11am, and the elderly driver was unharmed. Personnel from the Civil Defence Force responded to the incident and arrived at the scene to provide assistance It was also learnt that two individuals from the coffeeshop were sent to hospital by ambulance, possibly due to shock from the incident.

items.title: Honouring teachers as the pulse of the nation's future
items.summary: This year's theme is more than a mere symbolic expression - it represents a profound recognition of teachers as the driving force behind national development and societal progress. The Malaysia Education Blueprint (RPM) 2026–2035, launched by the MOE in January 2026, positions teachers as the primary drivers of national education transformation. Teachers' wellbeing remains a national priority.

items.title: More retention ponds to be built across KL to curb flash floods
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Kuala Lumpur City Hall (DBKL) is stepping up efforts to reduce flooding by building on-site retention ponds across selected green spaces in the city. Minister in the Prime Minister's Department (Federal Territories) Hannah Yeoh said DBKL has already begun constructing retention ponds at several sites and is identifying additional locations for new ponds. "DBKL is building on-site retention ponds to increase the number of ponds at sites such as the Botanical Gardens and Padang Merbok.

items.title: PKR sec-gen says Pasir Gudang MP does not speak for govt in resignation call over UEC varsity decision
items.summary: Yesterday, Hassan called on Education Minister Fadhlina Sidek and Higher Education Minister Datuk Seri Zambry Abd Kadir to resign, claiming they had failed to defend the national education policy involving public universities. The Higher Education Ministry had earlier announced that graduates from tahfiz schools, Chinese independent secondary schools and other institutions outside the national education system would be allowed to pursue selected programmes at public higher education institutions, subject to conditions. The move followed a Cabinet decision on admission pathways for students outside the national education system into public higher education institutions.

items.title: Anwar: Educators must accept criticism positively and show resilience in facing challenges
items.summary: IPOH, May 16 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said educators need to be big-hearted in accepting challenges and criticism positively, especially regarding shortcomings and problems involving students. He said education cannot be separated from being linked to personal, character, human or humanity and any problems that arise are often linked to schools until they sometimes burden educators. “Social problems certainly affect children in schools and certainly cause commotion.

items.title: Five siblings follow family legacy in teaching profession
items.summary: SK Sebako senior assistant for student affairs Mohamad Azwan Azahari said his parents, who each served as teachers for more than 30 years, were the main inspiration behind the siblings' interest in education from a young age. "When we were young, our parents would often bring students home to our quarters for extra classes. Azwan, 34, said the family quarters often become a gathering place for pupils, especially on weekends.

items.title: Overnight queues, RM50 runners and shattered hopes at KL Audemars Piguet x Swatch launch (VIDEO)
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 16 — By the time the sun rose over Mid Valley Megamall this morning, Ayu Omar and her daughter Allesya had already spent nearly 10 hours waiting for a watch from the new Audemars Piguet x Swatch “Royal Pop” collection. Overnight queue for limited drop “Firstly, I like Swatch. At around 9.15am, a Swatch staff member informed the crowd that only a limited number of watches were available.

items.title: Suspected drunk driver in Klang wrong-way crash to be charged Monday
items.summary: KLANG: A suspected drunk driver who drove against traffic and crashed into a motorcyclist, leaving the victim in critical condition, will be charged at the magistrate's court here on Monday. South Klang police chief Assistant Commissioner Lim Jit Huey said the investigation paper into the incident had been submitted to the deputy public prosecutor's office for further action. "The driver will be charged on Monday at the Klang magistrate's court," he said when contacted today.

items.title: Selangor launches Bantuan Rahmah Insan Istimewa to expand support for PwDs, says state exco
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, May 16 — The Selangor government today launched the Bantuan Rahmah Insan Istimewa Selangor (BRAIS) 2026 programme to expand existing support for the welfare of persons with disabilities (PwDs). State Women Development and Social Welfare Committee chairman Anfaal Saari said the initiative, under the Yayasan Anak Istimewa Selangor (YANIS), is a rebranding of the Bantuan Khas Anak Istimewa Selangor (ANIS) aimed at delivering more comprehensive and high-impact development for PwDs in the state. Elaborating further, Anfaal said that in addition to the new categories, BRAIS 2026 will retain existing support, including assistance for therapy centre fees (RM1,200), special equipment (up to RM3,000) and special education school fees (RM120).

items.title: Yeoh to meet MACC for more details on KL retention pond issue
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Minister in the Prime Minister's Department (Federal Territories) Hannah Yeoh wants to meet the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) over alleged irregularities involving the alienation of the Batu and Jinjang flood retention ponds. She said that although the MACC has denied any element of corruption in the transaction, she does not want to rely solely on its report and will arrange a meeting this week to obtain further details. "Just because the MACC has said there is no corruption involved, I don't want to rely solely on its report.

items.title: Economy minister: Rafizi's vending machine project helped the B40
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Economy Ministry today defended the People's Income Initiative – Food Entrepreneur Initiative (IPR-INSAN) vending machine programme, saying the initiative has shown proven success in boosting the income of poor and asnaf (underprivileged) participants. This comes despite calls for an Auditor-General's probe into the project championed by Economy Minister Datuk Seri Rafizi Ramli. Economy Minister Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir said the ministry's focus remained on ensuring the programme delivered tangible results for targeted groups rather than responding to external speculation.

items.title: Government boosts asnaf economy via Economy Ministry, MAIWP collaboration
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Asnaf groups will continue to remain a priority under the People's Income Initiative (IPR) despite current economic challenges and concerns over government spending, Economy Minister Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir said. The programme, he said, was specifically designed to assist low-income groups, particularly amid increasingly challenging economic conditions. The strategic collaboration, through the People's Income Initiative – Food Entrepreneur Initiative (IPR-INSAN), aims to strengthen the economic standing of asnaf groups and small entrepreneurs in the Federal Territories.

items.title: Education Ministry unveils 12 initiatives to empower teachers at national Teachers’ Day celebration
items.summary: Education Minister Fadhlina Sidek said the Google Certified Educator (GCE) Level 1 and Level 2 certification courses would continue to be offered free of charge to all teachers to support their professional development in digital literacy. She said the initiative would benefit teachers nationwide, with the ministry bearing the cost of RM40 per teacher for GCE Level 1 and RM100 per teacher for GCE Level 2. Fadhlina also said the ministry would offer a special Federal Training Award to teachers serving in schools in hardship areas including hospital classes, floating schools and rural schools in P1, P2 and P3 areas.

items.title: On Teachers’ Day, Anwar says educators crucial in shaping values amid complex global challenges
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 16 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said the role of teachers is becoming ever more crucial in an increasingly complex world, particularly in shaping a generation with strong moral character and inner strength grounded in human values. He said teachers’ responsibilities are no longer confined to imparting knowledge, but also include building confidence, upholding human dignity, and instilling hope and aspiration in life. Anwar said that without values and ethics, development would only result in progress without direction.

items.title: Fuziah: PKR MP's call for ministers to quit over UEC a 'personal opinion'
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: PKR secretary-general Datuk Dr Fuziah Salleh says Pasir Gudang member of parliament Hassan Abdul Karim's call for the higher education and education ministers to resign over the Unified Examination Certificate (UEC) issue is his personal opinion. Actually, this UEC issue is not a new issue. Yesterday, Hassan, who is PKR's Pasir Gudang member of parliament, called on Higher Education Minister Datuk Seri Dr Zambry Abdul Kadir and Education Minister Fadhlina Sidek to step down for allegedly failing to defend the national education policy.

items.title: Safety concerns near Empire Residence prompt halt to 26-storey condo project in Damansara Perdana
items.summary: PETALING JAYA, May 16 — Concerns over a hillslope condominium project behind Empire Residence in Damansara Perdana have prompted the Petaling Jaya City Council (MBPJ) to issue a stop-work order for the next two weeks. Damansara MP Gobind Singh Deo said the directive was issued following complaints from residents over mud and water flow during heavy rain, which they believe is originating from the construction site, The Star reported today. He said Class 3 slopes may be developed under strict conditions while Class 4 slopes are generally not allowed for permanent structures.

items.title: From used pencil and pen, gifts that still move retired teachers' hearts
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Years after leaving the classroom, three retired teachers still find themselves revisiting moments from their teaching journeys, including heartfelt gestures from their students during Teachers' Day celebrations. Muhammad Mustafa Abdul Salam, 58, said the most touching and unforgettable gift he ever received was a used pencil and eraser. Moved by his student's sincerity, Mustafa said he went on to buy the student new school supplies.

items.title: Trump says ISIS second-in-command Abu-Bilal al-Minuki eliminated
items.summary: BENGALURU: US President Donald Trump said on Friday that Abu-Bilal al-Minuki, second-in-command of ISIS globally, had been eliminated in an operation conducted by US and Nigerian forces. Abu-Bilal al-Minuki, second-in-command of ISIS globally, thought he could hide in Africa, but little did he know we had sources who kept us informed on what he was doing," Trump said in a post on Truth Social. The US had struck what it said were Islamist bases in northwestern Nigeria on Christmas Day last year, following Trump's accusations.

items.title: Abang Johari: Sarawak eyes 700 million Asean market with new Tanjung Embang Port
items.summary: KUCHING, May 16 — Sarawak is targeting a market of 700 million Asean residents through the development of the modern Tanjung Embang Port and strategic logistics infrastructure to strengthen the state’s position as a regional trade hub, said Sarawak Premier Tan Sri Abang Johari Openg. Officiating at the Kuching Port Authority (KPA) 65th Anniversary Celebration Dinner here last night, the Premier said that geographically, Sarawak is located at the heart of Asean. Abang Johari, who is also the Minister of Finance and New Economy, shared that operations at Senari Port will eventually relocate to Tanjung Embang Port, given that the former may eventually no longer be able to accommodate the increase in cargo resulting from Sarawak’s economic growth.

items.title: DBKL demolishes illegal food stall in Cheras after owner ignores eight-month notice
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: City hall demolished an unauthorised business structure in Cheras on Thursday after its owner failed to comply with enforcement notices issued nearly eight months ago. In a post on Facebook, DBKL said the structure at Jalan Senohong, Taman Bukit Ria, which was used for food business operations, was torn down on May 14 following the owner's failure to act on an enforcement and site vacation notice issued on Sept 23 last year and a follow-up visit on Oct 7. DBKL said the demolition and site clearance proceeded smoothly after the owner removed all goods and equipment from the premises in accordance with standard operating procedures prior to the operation.

items.title: Malaysia must make clear Jho Low should face justice, says Gobind
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: DAP chairman Gobind Singh Deo says Malaysia must make clear that fugitive financier Low Taek Jho, or Jho Low, should face justice over the 1Malaysia Development Bhd (1MDB) scandal, amid reports of his bid for a presidential pardon in the United States. Gobind said while he accepted Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim's view that the pardon was entirely for the US government to decide , Malaysia must still make its stance clear. Gobind also said Low must face charges in Malaysia regardless of developments abroad.

items.title: KK court orders man to enter defence in sexual assault case involving 17‑year‑old girl
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, May 16 — The Sessions Court here has ordered a man to enter his defence on a charge of committing physical sexual assault against a teenage girl. Judge Amir Shah Amir Hassan made the order on the 63-year-old after ruling that the prosecution had established a prima facie case against him. Deputy public prosecutor Bryan Francis handled the prosecution while the accused was represented by counsel Mohd Al Hafidz Mohd Lokman.

items.title: Workload cut: Manual co-curricular record-keeping for schools abolished
items.summary: IPOH: The Education Ministry has abolished manual co-curricular record-keeping in a move aimed at reducing teachers' administrative workload and allowing them to focus more on teaching and students. Fadhlina said schools would be given autonomy to manage co-curricular records before entering students' data into the Physical, Sports and Co-Curricular Assessment System. We want teachers to focus on educational initiatives, classrooms and students.

items.title: Sepanggar body in bin case: Court grants extra month for suspect’s psychiatric report
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, May 16 — The Magistrates’ Court here has allowed a one-month extension for the mental evaluation of a fishmonger charged with murdering a woman, whose body parts were found at a communal rubbish bin in Indah Permai Sepanggar last February. Magistrate Dzul Elmy Yunus granted the application for Piluta Samad, 71, after the prosecution informed the court that his psychiatric report was not ready. Piluta’s legal team led by Datuk Ram Singh raised no objection to the extension request.

items.title: Elderly sisters killed, teen injured in hit-and-run
items.summary: SHAH ALAM: An elderly woman and her sister, both pedestrians, were killed while another person was injured in a hit-and-run at Jalan Pekan Baru, Bandar Baru Klang, yesterday. North Klang district police chief Assistant Commissioner S. Vijaya Rao said the two women were aged 57 and 62, while the injured victim is a 17-year-old boy, who is the son of one of the women. He said the North Klang district police headquarters' Traffic Complaint Counter received information on the accident at 8.14pm.

items.title: One-way ticket home: 28 foreign scammers to be deported
items.summary: TAWAU: Twenty-eight foreigners convicted of involvement in "love" and online investment scams will be deported by the Immigration Department. Tawau police chief Assistant Commissioner Jasmin Hussin said the suspects were arrested during a raid on four luxury bungalows on May 6. He said the syndicate conducted online scams targeting South Koreans via WhatsApp and Kakao Talk.

items.title: Police to boost sensitivity training for handling autism cases
items.summary: He said the police force remained committed to supporting the autism community through inclusive initiatives, awareness efforts and continued backing for the Pengaman-PDRM Autism Centre. Khalid said the centre's establishment in August 2020 also demonstrated that the responsibilities of the police extend beyond law enforcement duties. Khalid said he hopes that the Pengaman-PDRM Autism Centre would one day emerge as a leading regional centre for autism intervention.

items.title: Kelantan police remand married couple as probe continues into Bachok murder of disabled man
items.summary: KOTA BHARU, May 16 — A married couple has been remanded for seven and five days respectively to assist in the investigation into the murder of a disabled man at Ladang Merdeka Cherbu, Padang Lati, Bachok, in April. The 26-year-old male suspect has been remanded for seven days until next Friday, while his 27-year-old wife has been remanded for five days until Wednesday. The remand order was issued by Magistrate Fakhrul Razzi Abd Hamid at the Magistrate’s Court here to facilitate investigations under Section 302 of the Penal Code.

items.title: ‘Sandwich generation’ relief: EPF i-Legasi enables retirement savings transfers to family
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 16 — The Employees Provident Fund’s (EPF) newly introduced i-Legasi facility could provide much-needed financial planning support for Malaysia’s ‘sandwich generation’, who often juggle the responsibilities of caring for ageing parents while raising children. Amid rising living costs and longer life expectancy, the facility allows eligible members to transfer part of their retirement savings to immediate family members, such as spouses or children, to support long-term family financial planning. Nik Maheran said initiatives such as i-Legasi could encourage earlier discussions on retirement and family financial planning, adding that intergenerational support remains a deeply rooted aspect of Asian family values.

items.title: Female bus driver loses RM537,000 in online stock investment scam
items.summary: Seri Alam police chief Assistant Commissioner Mohd Sohaimi Ishak said the woman claimed to have been cheated after being deceived by an offer on Facebook promising double profits. He said the incident began in March when the victim saw a stock investment advertisement on the social media platform and clicked the provided link. "The victim was promised high profits in a short period.

items.title: [UPDATED] Anwar on UEC: From refusing to speak Malay 40 years ago, Chinese school mindsets have shifted
items.summary: IPOH: The government's latest decision involving Unified Examination Certificate (UEC) students was made possible following significant changes in attitudes and acceptance over the past four decades, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said. Anwar said discussions on recognising UEC graduates first began during his tenure as education minister about 40 years ago, when Chinese independent schools were largely detached from the national education system. Anwar said the situation has since changed significantly, with Chinese independent schools now showing greater alignment with the national education framework.

items.title: Mara to offer cadet pilot scholarships for STEM students, with priority for tahfiz school leavers
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Majlis Amanah Rakyat (Mara) will soon open applications for its cadet pilot programme, offering financial sponsorship to qualified students to undergo flight training at selected academies. "Mara will release the official advertisement along with the eligibility requirements for this cadet pilot programme very soon," Mara chairman Datuk Dr Asyraf Wajdi Dusuki said in a Facebook post. Asyraf added that academies receiving Mara sponsorship must have existing agreements with airlines to guarantee employment opportunities for trainees upon completion of their course.

items.title: Man in critical condition after shark attack in western Australia
items.summary: SYDNEY: A man in his thirties was in a "critical" condition Saturday after being attacked by a shark in western Australia, rescue services told AFP. The incident took place after 10am (0200 GMT) off Rottnest Island near the city of Perth, a spokesperson for St John Ambulance Western Australia said. It was the city's third recent shark-related death.

items.title: How a Philippine war crimes suspect thwarted arrest, for now
items.summary: Philippine Senator Ronald "Bato" Dela Rosa, wanted by the International Criminal Court for his alleged role in the country's deadly drug war, fled into the Manila night hours after an unprecedented shootout on Wednesday in the Senate where he had spent days holed up evading arrest. It was the second run-in involving dela Rosa in a week, after the senator escaped NBI agents in a cat-and-mouse chase. Dela Rosa, a 64-year-old former police chief, is unrepentant about his role in the drug war and previously said he would happily accompany Duterte to The Hague where the former leader faces a trial for crimes against humanity.

items.title: Sabah raid uncovers wildlife hoard of rhino horns, pangolin scales, elephant bones in former ranger’s Tuaran home
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 16 — A man believed to be a former forest ranger was arrested after allegedly possessing various parts of protected wildlife, estimated to be worth RM589,230, in a raid in Tuaran, Sabah, yesterday. Bukit Aman Internal Security and Public Order Department’s Wildlife Crime Bureau/Special Intelligence Investigation (WCB/PSK) commander Datuk Mohd Zaki Ashar Datuk Mohd Zaki Ashar said the 61-year-old man was arrested around 7.15 am in an operation resulting from the collaboration between WCB/PSK and the Sabah Wildlife Department. He said the case is being investigated under Section 41(1) of the Sabah Wildlife Conservation Enactment 1997 for possessing or hunting protected wildlife, as well as Section 41(2) of the same enactment for possessing or selling protected wildlife.

items.title: Immigration rounds up 54 illegal immigrants in Sabah crackdown
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU: The Sabah Immigration Department rounded up 54 foreigners for various immigration offences during enforcement operations in Sipitang and Semporna recently. The foreigners were detained when the department carried out operations codenamed Op Sapu and Op Kutip, a crackdown against undocumented migrants in Sabah. Meanwhile, in Semporna, four Pakistani men were detained following inspections on six people during simultaneous Op Sapu and Op Kutip operations.

items.title: [UPDATED] Bukit Aman probing claims of Jho Low's 'secret meetings' in Malaysia [WATCH]
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Police are investigating reports that fugitive financier Low Taek Jho, or Jho Low, returned to Malaysia for a "secret meeting" linked to 1Malaysia Development Bhd (1MDB). Inspector-General of Police Datuk Seri Mohd Khalid Ismail said police had yet to verify the claims. "We will investigate whether the meeting actually took place," he said after attending the police's Autism Day celebration at the Police Training Centre.

items.title: Beckham becomes first British billionaire sportsman
items.summary: LONDON: Former Manchester United F.C. and England star David Beckham has become Britain's first billionaire sportsman, according to the 2026 Sunday Times Rich List. Beckham and his wife Victoria Beckham's collective wealth reached an estimated £1.185 billion (US$1.583 billion) this year, the Rich List compilers said.

items.title: Taiping pair convicted as teens for killing girl’s brother remain under indefinite detention after court rejects bail bid
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 16 — The Court of Appeal today dismissed applications by two former students, a boy and a girl, seeking a stay of execution and bail pending the hearing of their appeal. They had appealed against a High Court decision that found them guilty of murder and ordered them to be detained at the pleasure of the Sultan of Perak. Justice Datuk Ahmad Kamal Md Shahid, who chaired a three-member bench, ruled that the reasons advanced by both applicants did not constitute special or exceptional circumstances to justify granting bail.

items.title: Starbucks to lay off hundreds of employees in US
items.summary: ISTANBUL: Starbucks announced on Friday that it will lay off 300 employees in the United States (US), around 3 per cent of its labour force in the country, Anadolu Ajansi (AA) reported, citing FOX. "As a result, we're eliminating approximately 300 US support roles," it noted. The company is also closing some regional support offices.

items.title: Teachers architects of future generation, says Anwar
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The role of teachers has become more critical than ever in shaping a generation with wisdom, strong morals and values amid increasingly complex global challenges, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said. Anwar said a teacher's duty and responsibility is more than just educating their students, but also building their confidence and nurturing them to uphold dignity. "Happy Teachers' Day - Guru Bitara Nadi Negara."

items.title: Married couple arrested in disabled man's murder probe
items.summary: KOTA BHARU: Police arrested a married couple yesterday evening to assist in the investigation into the murder of a disabled man in Padang Lati, Bachok, in April. The 26-year-old male suspect was remanded for seven days today, while his wife, 27, was remanded for five days. Bachok police chief Superintendent Mohamad Ismail Jamaluddin said the suspects were arrested at a house in Kampung Padang Salim at 4.30pm yesterday.

items.title: Weeks after broadcaster Karam Singh Walia’s death, eldest daughter dies in her sleep at 44
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 16 — Barely having recovered from the loss of renowned broadcast journalist Datuk Karam Singh Walia on April 6, his family has been struck by another tragedy after his eldest daughter passed away on Wednesday morning (May 13). News of the passing of Sharonjit Kaur Walia, 44, was confirmed by her husband, Kashminder Singh Sandhu, who said she died in her sleep at their residence at D’Palma @ Taman Pinang Gading, Sikamat, Seremban, Negeri Sembilan. News of the lecturer’s passing was also shared on Wednesday by Politeknik Port Dickson, Negeri Sembilan.

items.title: King and Queen pay tribute to teachers for shaping Malaysia’s future generations
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 16 — Their Majesties Sultan Ibrahim and Raja Zarith Sofiah, King and Queen of Malaysia, today extended Teachers’ Day greetings to all educators in the country in appreciation of their contributions and sacrifices in educating the nation’s children. In a post on Sultan Ibrahim Sultan Iskandar’s Facebook page, Their Majesties also expressed appreciation to teachers, who serve as a vital pillar in shaping knowledgeable, well-mannered and competitive generations for the nation’s future. “Sultan Ibrahim and Raja Zarith Sofiah express their appreciation for the sacrifices, patience and dedication of teachers in imparting knowledge and instilling noble values in the younger generation,” the post said.

items.title: MetMalaysia warns of short-term thunderstorms with intense rainfall in Perlis, Kedah, Penang and parts of Sabah, Johor
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 16 — Thunderstorms, heavy rain and strong winds are expected to hit all northern states in Peninsular Malaysia as well as several areas in Pahang, Johor, Sabah and Labuan until noon today, according to the Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia). In a weather warning issued at 9am, MetMalaysia said the conditions are forecast to affect the whole of Perlis, Kedah and Penang, as well as Labuan. In Perak, the affected areas are expected to include Kerian, Larut, Matang dan Selama, Hulu Perak and Kuala Kangsar.

items.title: Govt says wider IPTA access for UEC, tahfiz students not a recognition of education systems
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 16 — The decision to open admission pathways for students from foreign education systems, including the Unified Examination Certificate (UEC), to public institutions of higher learning (IPTA) does not imply recognition of those systems, the Ministry of Higher Education (MOHE) said. Higher Education director-general Datuk Prof Dr Azlinda Azman said the move was a government initiative to widen access to higher education across all identified levels. Our access to education is clearly outlined in the Malaysian Higher Education Blueprint (RPTM) 2026-2035.

items.title: Malaysia-led Gaza aid flotilla enters high-risk waters as interception fears grow
items.summary: SEPANG, May 16 — The Sumud Nusantara Command Centre (SNCC) has confirmed that 54 ships of the Global Sumud Flotilla (GSF) are currently in yellow zone 2, which is approximately 340 nautical miles from the Gaza coast. This time the GSF mission involves nearly 500 international participants from 45 countries mobilised by various humanitarian activist groups around the world. Sani Araby meanwhile said SNCC also officially launched four efforts through the Sumud Protocol as a phase one action plan that was mobilised starting from the final phase of the flotilla mission to Gaza.

items.title: Heroin, meth, tobacco, phones and cash found inside Ipoh police lock-up, internal investigation underway
items.summary: IPOH, May 16 — Perak police have launched an internal investigation into the discovery of items suspected to be drugs and mobile phones in a lock-up cell at the Central Police Station of the Ipoh District Police Headquarters (IPD) last Tuesday. “Perak police view seriously the discovery of prohibited items, including suspected drugs and mobile phones, in the lock-up following an inspection conducted by the Integrity and Standards Compliance Department (JIPS) of the Perak Police Contingent Headquarters (IPK),” he said in a statement yesterday. Noor Hisam said the inspection, conducted at about 10 pm on May 12, involved a cell housing four detainees.

items.title: No automatic vacancy for Kijal seat as Razali’s fine does not exceed RM2,000, says Terengganu Speaker
items.summary: KUALA TERENGGANU, May 16 — Datuk Razali Idris’ position as the Kijal state assemblyman is secure and there will be no automatic vacancy, said Terengganu State Assembly Speaker Datuk Mohd Nor Hamzah. He said the issue of the Kijal state seat position arose following the Sessions Court’s decision to fine Razali RM2,000 after he was found guilty of making inflammatory statements during his speech at the Kemaman By-Election Machinery Launching Ceremony, three years ago. Bernama yesterday reported that Razali, who is also the chairman of the Terengganu State Tourism, Culture, Environment and Climate Change Committee, withdrew his appeal against the Sessions Court’s decision to impose a fine of RM2,000 after finding him guilty of issuing a seditious statement during his speech at the Kemaman By-Election Machinery Launching Ceremony, three years ago.

items.title: Zambry wants student startups to go global under new higher education entrepreneurship plan
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 16 — The Ministry of Higher Education (MOHE) launched the Higher Education Institution Entrepreneurship Action Plan (PTK-IPT) 2026-2030 yesterday. The plan aims to build a sustainable, inclusive, and high-impact entrepreneurship ecosystem across universities to produce globally competitive, holistic entrepreneurs. Minister Datuk Seri Dr. Zambry Abd Kadir said the plan aligns with the Malaysia Higher Education Plan 2026-2035, which prioritises educational flexibility, tech mastery, lifelong learning, innovation and future talent development.

items.title: Govt rolls out two-day Rukun Negara camps for youths aged 18 to 30 starting this month
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 16 — The Rukun Negara Youth Tour 2026 programme will implemented from this month to October, involving 15 selected higher learning institutions nationwide in an effort to strengthen unity, patriotism and self-confidence of Malaysian youth. National Unity Minister Datuk Aaron Ago Dagang said the programme was implemented through an interactive and experience-based approach to strengthen appreciation of the values of the Rukun Negara in daily life. Aaron also included an infographic on the programme organised by the Ministry of National Unity through the Department of National Unity and Integration, which targets the participation of youth, aged 18 to 30.

items.title: ‘Profits’ looked real until withdrawals failed: Two in Penang lose RM2.7m to China-linked investment schemes
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, May 16 — Two senior citizens including a former company director lost a total of almost RM2.7 million after being duped by online investment syndicates that promised lucrative returns through separate investment platforms, recently. Penang police chief Datuk Azizee Ismail said the first case involved a 62-year-old man who worked as a machinery attendant and suffered a loss of RM1.294 million after being scammed in an online investment scheme known as “Global Finance Trends XA”. In a separate case, he said a former female company director, 72, also lost RM1.403 million after being scammed by an online investment syndicate using the name “Shanghai Futures Exchange”.

items.title: Pangkor migrant boat sinking: Authorities now believe 39 were onboard, raising fears more may still be missing
items.summary: IPOH, May 16 — Four more bodies were found today from the incident involving a boat carrying Indonesian migrants that capsized in the waters off Pulau Pangkor, bringing the death toll to 16 so far. Perak Malaysian Maritime Enforcement Agency (MMEA) director Maritime Captain Mohamad Shukri Khotob said three of the victims found were men and were discovered about 2.5 nautical miles southwest of Pulau Buloh, while another body, whose identity has yet to be confirmed, was found about 21.4 nautical miles southwest of Pulau Sangga Kechil. “Meanwhile, the other three victims were found by the Royal Malaysian Navy (RMN) at about 5.45 pm,” he said when contacted by Bernama yesterday.

items.title: Ipoh junction now rocking ‘light saber’ traffic lights, drivers say it’s way safer (VIDEO)
items.summary: IPOH, May 16 — A striking new “light saber” traffic light system has appeared near Enclave Residences along Jalan Sultan Azlan Shah, following a spate of accidents allegedly linked to poor visibility at the junction. Mohammad Nizar said the illuminated traffic light system, the first of its kind to be installed in Ipoh and the Kinta district, complies with existing traffic light specifications and standards. “Taking into account complaints received regarding accidents allegedly caused by the lack of visibility of the existing traffic light system, the Public Works Department (JKR) introduced an innovative initiative by installing the ‘light saber’ style traffic light pole.

items.title: Applications open June 30 as Putrajaya gives non‑mainstream students direct shot at public universities
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 15 — Applications for students from outside the national education system to pursue studies at public institutions of higher learning (IPTA) will open on June 30. Higher Education director-general Datuk Prof Dr Azlinda Azman said that for this year, applications will be submitted directly to the respective universities before the process is integrated into the UPUOnline system in 2027. “InsyaAllah, we will open applications directly through the universities offering these programmes beginning June 30 to enable applications to be submitted this year,” she said at a special press conference on admission pathways for students from outside the national education system to public universities here today.

items.title: Sabah’s Lahad Datu–Tawau road collapse strands vehicles, diversion works underway at Jalan Silam
items.summary: LAHAD DATU, May 15 — The main road linking Lahad Datu and Tawau was cut off this evening after a 50-metre stretch of Jalan Silam here collapsed in both directions. Lahad Datu District Officer Firuz Idzualdeen @ Benetty Mohd Dzul said the road was temporarily impassable and there is currently no alternative route to either Tawau or Lahad Datu. — Bernama
